zpl.importTooltip: Paste ZPL code and turn it into editable objects
zpl.exportTooltip: Generate ZPL code from this label
zpl.importHelp: Paste a ZPL/ZPL II label. Elements become editable objects on the canvas; unrecognised commands are preserved as-is.
zpl.importReplaceWarning: Import replaces the objects on the canvas but keeps the current label size — change it with the size button.
zpl.overflowHint: Content reaches {{w}}×{{h}} mm — larger than the {{lw}}×{{lh}} mm label. The ZPL may use a different DPI: try switching it, or resize the label.
zpl.exportHelp: Live ZPL for the current label. Variables like {NAZWA} and ^PQ{NoLabel} are kept intact for your external software.
zpl.batchHelp: Fill {{column}} placeholders from a dataset — one label per row, concatenated into a single .zpl.
zpl.invalidZpl: This doesn't look like ZPL. A label must contain ^XA at the start and ^XZ at the end — check that you didn't paste e.g. an HTML page or a PDF file.

tspl.exportTooltip: Export this label as TSPL for TSC / Toshiba printers
tspl.exportHelp: Live TSPL for the current label, generated for TSC / Toshiba-compatible printers.

labelSize.title: Label size
labelSize.help: Set the printable label size in millimetres. Object positions are unchanged.

auth.changePasswordIntro: Your account requires setting a new password. Pick one at least 10 characters long.

errors.csrfFailed: Session expired — please refresh the page
errors.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 10 characters
errors.newPasswordSameAsOld: New password must differ from the current one
errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.comingSoon: Open Templates to design your first label.

cards.templatesBody: Create a new template, or open an existing one to edit and generate PDFs.
cards.helpBody: App walkthrough, common questions, and troubleshooting.

templates.name: Template name
templates.empty: You don't have any templates yet. Click "New template" to start.
templates.exportTooltip: Download this template as a .blg-template.json file
templates.importStep2: Review settings and choose which objects to import.
templates.importErrorBadFile: Couldn't read the file — is it a .blg-template.json export?
templates.shareHint: Everyone signed in will see this template in the Library and can clone it ("Use"). Only you can edit it.
templates.featuredHint: Preview image shown on the list card and in the Library (PNG/JPG/SVG, max 5 MB).

editor.selectToEdit: Select an element on the canvas to edit its properties.
editor.multiSelected: {{count}} objects selected. Use the alignment bar above the canvas.
editor.backgroundHint: Uploads an image as locked background — visible only in the editor, not in the PDF.
editor.textBlockSample: Type a longer text here to see wrapping
editor.overflowReportedAtRender: Overflow warnings appear during PDF generation (not on the canvas).
editor.pdfWarningsTooltip: PDF was generated but some text didn't fit in its block.
editor.dynamicHint: Use {{column_name}} to insert a dynamic field (replaced by CSV/Excel data in the batch generator) or {{date+14d}} for a date computed at generation time.
editor.datePreviewTooltip: Date field — computed at generation time (preview uses your browser's date)
editor.saveFirstHint: Save the template before generating a series

series.title: Generate series
series.dateAutoHint: Optional — today's date is used if unmapped
series.uploadIntro: Upload a CSV/Excel file (max 1000 rows, 10 MB) or a SQLite .db/.sqlite (max 50 MB).
series.sqliteIntro: SQLite file detected. Pick a table or write a custom SELECT query.
series.sqliteNoTables: This file contains no user tables.
series.sqliteQueryPlaceholder: SELECT col1, col2 FROM table WHERE …
series.sqliteAdvancedHint: Single SELECT only (max 1000 rows). The connection is read-only; ATTACH, PRAGMA, INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DROP are blocked.
series.mapIntro: Map each dynamic field in the template to a spreadsheet column. Fields whose name matches a column auto-map.
series.noPlaceholders: This template has no dynamic fields — every row will produce an identical label.
series.filterSkipHint: Without a filter, {{count}} labels will be generated.

users.title: User management
users.tempPasswordHelp: Min. 10 characters. The user will be forced to change it on first login.
users.tempPasswordCreatedBody: Share this temporary password with the user — it won't be shown again:

errors.emailExists: A user with this email already exists
errors.cannotDisableSelf: You can't disable your own account

devices.title: Devices (print connector)
devices.intro: The blg-connector is a small program installed on a computer inside the local network with your label printers. Create a device, copy its token and paste it into the connector's configuration — the agent will start polling the server for print jobs.
devices.createHint: After creating you'll get an access token — shown only once.
devices.name: Device name
devices.confirmDelete: Delete device "{{name}}"? Its token will stop working.
devices.tokenCreatedBody: Copy the token and paste it into the connector's configuration. For security it cannot be shown again — if you lose it, delete the device and create a new one.
devices.empty: No devices yet. Add one to connect your label printers to the app.

errors.nameTaken: You already have a device with this name.

wizard.title: Connect a printer
wizard.osQuestion: Which computer is the printer connected to?
wizard.osHint: We detected your system — if it's right, just pick it.
wizard.nameHint: Just a label so you recognise it later — e.g. "Office Mac".
wizard.nameError: That name is taken — try another.
wizard.installHint: One file, one click — the installer sets everything up and starts the program in the background.

installRun.mac: 1. Open Terminal (Cmd+Space, type Terminal). 2. Click Copy below. 3. Paste into Terminal and press Enter — the installer does the rest and verifies the connection itself.
installRun.windows: Run the downloaded file. If the blue SmartScreen window appears: More info → Run anyway.
installRun.linux: In a terminal: `bash ~/Downloads/blg-connect.sh` (the only command — the installer does the rest).

wizard.tokenPrivacy: Your private token lives inside the downloaded installer file — don't share that file with anyone.
wizard.waiting: Waiting for your computer to check in…
wizard.waitingHint: The installer finishes in the background — this usually takes up to a minute.
wizard.notSeen: I can't see your computer yet
wizard.tip1: Did the installer finish with its success message ("OK - gotowe")? If not, run it again.
wizard.tip2: Is Tailscale on and connected (menu-bar icon)?
wizard.tip3: Check the log: macOS — connector.log file in ~/Library/Application Support/blg-connector; Windows — %LOCALAPPDATA%\blg-connector\connector.log; Linux — journalctl --user -u blg-connector.service.
wizard.connectedHint: "{{name}}" is online now. You can print labels from the editor.
wizard.printersHint: This list fills in by itself once the program finds printers connected to this computer.
wizard.printersEmpty: I can't see any printers yet — plug in a USB printer and add it in your system settings, I'll refresh the list myself.
wizard.downloadWithPrinter: Download the installer with this printer
wizard.rerunHint: run it again — it will update the configuration
wizard.virtualLink: Printing from another program (e.g. Word, Excel)? Set up a virtual printer →
wizard.virtualHintUnix: Print from any program as if it were a regular printer — the printout lands straight in this app's Inbox.
wizard.virtualWinStep1: Download and install the Zebra ZDesigner driver from zebra.com.
wizard.virtualWinStep2: Add a new printer: port "Standard TCP/IP", address 127.0.0.1, port 9101.
wizard.virtualWinStep3: In the port settings, turn off SNMP and set the data type to RAW.
wizard.virtualWinStep4: Done — print from any program, the printout lands in this app's Inbox.

print.title: Print via connector
print.tooltip: Send the label (ZPL) to a printer through the local connector
print.printerManualHint: The agent hasn't reported its printer list yet — type the name from its config.yaml.
print.queued: Job queued — waiting for the agent to pick it up…
print.sentToAgent: Agent picked up the job — printing…
print.deviceOfflineHint: This device is offline — the job will wait in the queue until the agent connects.
print.noDevices: You don't have a connector device yet. Connect one on the Devices page:
print.localHint: Connector detected on this computer — the label goes straight to the printer, skipping the server queue.

captures.title: Inbox — captured labels
captures.intro: Labels printed from other applications to the connector's virtual printer land here. Open one in the editor (elements become editable), copy the ZPL, or delete it.
captures.empty: No captured labels yet. Configure the virtual printer in the agent's config.yaml (capture section) and print something from any application.

folders.title: Folders
folders.confirmDelete: Delete folder "{{name}}"? Its templates stay (moved to "No folder").

errors.nameTaken: You already have a folder with this name.

library.title: Library
library.intro: Ready-made starter projects and templates shared by other users. "Use" creates your own copy and opens it in the editor.
library.emptyShared: Nothing shared yet. Share yours: Templates → ⚙ on a card → "Share in the Library".

history.title: Version history
history.tooltip: Saved template versions — preview and restore
history.intro: Each manual save (Save / Ctrl+S) creates a version; autosave does not. You can restore any of them — the current state is then kept in history as a new version.
history.empty: No saved versions yet. Click Save to create the first.
history.confirmRestore: Restore version v{{version}}? The current state will be saved to history.

historyPage.intro: Generated files (single labels and series) — re-downloadable for 30 days.
historyPage.empty: No generated files yet. Generate a PDF from the editor or a series from a dataset.
